title: rubus hirtus subsp. declivis
intro: Rubus hirtus subsp. declivis, also known as the declivis thimbleberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus hirtus plant, a type of thimbleberry that is native to the mountains of Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus subsp. declivis include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to help manage digestive issues and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
